title: Slowly compounding, one good decision at a time
author: Independent_Gur8648
selftext:
Finished May up about $132k.

Looking at the calendar, the green days stand out, but honestly the biggest lesson came from the red ones. Although those three days on which I lost -$1.06K. -$22.8K. and -$39.5K. respectively were painful. it was precisely these experiences that kept me alert. preventing me from becoming reckless due to overconfidence.

Most of my trades this month came from the same playbook:Trading strength instead of predicting reversals. Waiting for confirmation before sizing up. Taking partial profits into momentum spikes. Cutting trades quickly when my thesis breaks

One thing that helped a lot was staying focused on a few names instead of chasing everything moving on the scanner. Some days I only took 1–2 setups and called it done.

The goal was never to hit a specific dollar amount. The goal was to execute the same process every day and let the numbers take care of themselves.

May ended at +$132k. Plenty of mistakes, plenty to improve.

Now it's time to see what June has in store.
